title: Support external network namespaces from podman run command.
body:
A long time ago I opened a PR to support setting up a network namespace outside of Docker and have the container created by Docker join that network. 

https://github.com/docker/libnetwork/pull/372.

Upstream Docker rejected this... :^(
Let's get it into podman

Basically

podman run --net path:/PATH/TO/NETNS fedora sh

comments.body: @baude You want me to give this to someone else?  Like @haircommander?
@baude Ever get a chance to look at this?
@rhatdan @mheon I am not quite sure, but I'm pretty sure --net ns:<path> works for this purpose. I just added the path option on a branch, but is it necessary with ns?
@haircommander I think we didn't have `--net=ns:` when this was written - I think that we can close this since it's been added

I agree with @mheon. Joining another network NS is working since commit 41c7e43b4dbb. Looking at the manpage, I think that some of the help messages could be a bit more informative but we can address that in a more organized fashion.
